q: Does your digital calendar accurately reflect all your upcoming university deadlines?
What is your typical reaction when a task notification pops up on your phone?
Is the 'Snooze' button a frequent choice for your deadline reminders?
Can you access your schedule instantly from any device you own?
To what extent do you trust your digital alerts over your own memory?
When was the last time you reviewed your upcoming weekly tasks?
Does your calendar use different colors to separate study, work, and life?
If a professor changes a deadline, how fast is it updated in your app?
Have you ever missed a submission because a reminder was set too late?
Are you currently juggling too many different planning apps at once?
Do you utilize 'Travel Time' alerts to arrive at meetings on time?
Is it your habit to set multiple reminders for a single high-stakes task?
How often do you block out specific hours for 'Deep Work' on your grid?
Do you feel 'Notification Fatigue' from too many calendar pings?
When do you usually clear out old or cancelled events from your view?
Is your digital calendar shared with classmates for group projects?
Has a technical glitch (like no sync) ever caused you to miss a class?
Do you find the 'Widget' on your home screen helpful for quick checks?
Are location maps and Zoom links attached to your calendar entries?
Has using a digital system actually made you a more punctual person?
